requirements.title: ValidateFrame rejects ID > 0x3F
requirements.description: ValidateFrame shall return an error when Frame.ID exceeds MaxID (0x3F).
requirements.rationale: SG-05: prevents an invalid identifier from being transmitted or processed.

requirements.title: ValidateFrame rejects empty data
requirements.description: ValidateFrame shall return an error when Frame.Data has zero length.
requirements.rationale: SG-05: a zero-length LIN frame is malformed and has no defined wire format.

requirements.title: ValidateFrame rejects oversized data
requirements.description: ValidateFrame shall return an error when len(Frame.Data) exceeds MaxDataLen (8).
requirements.rationale: SG-05: LIN 2.x limits payloads to 8 bytes; exceeding this is a protocol violation.

requirements.title: ProtectID parity bit P0
requirements.description: ProtectID shall compute P0 = ID0 XOR ID1 XOR ID2 XOR ID4 and place it in bit 6 of the returned PID byte.
requirements.rationale: SG-01: P0 is mandated by LIN 2.x §2.3.1 for frame identifier error detection.

requirements.title: ProtectID parity bit P1
requirements.description: ProtectID shall compute P1 = NOT(ID1 XOR ID3 XOR ID4 XOR ID5) and place it in bit 7 of the returned PID byte.
requirements.rationale: SG-01: P1 is mandated by LIN 2.x §2.3.1 for frame identifier error detection.

requirements.title: VerifyPID accepts correct parity
requirements.description: VerifyPID shall return the raw 6-bit ID and nil error when the parity bits in the PID are correct.
requirements.rationale: SG-01: correct PID passes without false rejection.

requirements.title: VerifyPID rejects incorrect parity
requirements.description: VerifyPID shall return an error when the parity bits in the supplied PID do not match those computed by ProtectID for the embedded 6-bit ID.
requirements.rationale: SG-01: a corrupted PID must be detected and rejected.

requirements.title: Classic checksum covers data bytes only
requirements.description: CalcChecksum with ClassicChecksum shall sum the data bytes only (excluding PID) using inverted carry-around 8-bit addition.
requirements.rationale: SG-02: LIN 1.x classic checksum algorithm per LIN spec §2.3.2.

requirements.title: Enhanced checksum includes PID
requirements.description: CalcChecksum with EnhancedChecksum shall include the PID byte in the sum alongside the data bytes, using inverted carry-around 8-bit addition.
requirements.rationale: SG-02: LIN 2.x enhanced checksum algorithm per LIN spec §2.3.2.

requirements.title: Checksum carry-around inversion
requirements.description: CalcChecksum shall apply carry-around addition (sum > 0xFF wraps by subtracting 0xFF, not 0x100) and invert the final sum (0xFF - sum).
requirements.rationale: SG-02: correct carry-around is required for interoperability with LIN hardware.

requirements.title: Bus.Publish registers slave response
requirements.description: Bus.Publish shall register a response payload for the given frame ID so that the next SendHeader for that ID returns the registered data.
requirements.rationale: Core interface contract: slave response registration.

requirements.title: Bus.Subscribe delivers matching frames
requirements.description: Bus.Subscribe shall return a channel that receives every Frame whose ID matches any supplied Filter, or all Frames when no filters are supplied.
requirements.rationale: Core interface contract: frame observation.

requirements.title: MasterBus.SendHeader returns synthesised Frame
requirements.description: MasterBus.SendHeader shall trigger a frame exchange for the given ID and return the resulting Frame including correct PID and checksum.
requirements.rationale: Core interface contract: master-driven frame exchange.

requirements.title: MasterBus.SendHeader returns ErrNoResponse
requirements.description: MasterBus.SendHeader shall return ErrNoResponse when no slave has registered a response for the requested frame ID.
requirements.rationale: Absence of a slave response is a distinct, detectable condition.

requirements.title: ValidateFrame accepts ID = 0x3F
requirements.description: ValidateFrame shall return nil error when Frame.ID equals MaxID (0x3F), the boundary maximum.
requirements.rationale: SG-05: boundary condition — ID 0x3F is a valid LIN identifier and must not be falsely rejected.

requirements.title: ValidateFrame accepts data length 1
requirements.description: ValidateFrame shall return nil error when len(Frame.Data) equals 1, the boundary minimum.
requirements.rationale: SG-05: boundary condition — a 1-byte payload is the smallest valid LIN frame.

requirements.title: ValidateFrame accepts data length 8
requirements.description: ValidateFrame shall return nil error when len(Frame.Data) equals MaxDataLen (8), the boundary maximum.
requirements.rationale: SG-05: boundary condition — an 8-byte payload is the largest valid LIN frame.

requirements.title: ProtectID preserves lower 6 bits
requirements.description: For any id in 0x00–0x3F, (ProtectID(id) & 0x3F) shall equal id.
requirements.rationale: SG-01: the PID must carry the original ID in bits 0–5 so receivers can extract it.

requirements.title: Bus.Publish with nil removes registration
requirements.description: Bus.Publish(id, nil) shall remove any previously registered response for id; subsequent SendHeader for that id shall return ErrNoResponse.
requirements.rationale: Slaves must be able to withdraw a response (e.g., entering sleep mode) without restarting the bus.

requirements.title: Bus.Subscribe with All=true delivers every frame
requirements.description: A Filter with All=true shall match every Frame regardless of ID; Bus.Subscribe with such a filter delivers all frames on the bus.
requirements.rationale: Bus monitoring and logging require all-frame visibility.

requirements.title: ErrNoResponse is a non-nil sentinel error
requirements.description: ErrNoResponse shall be a non-nil error value that callers can test with errors.Is(err, lin.ErrNoResponse).
requirements.rationale: Callers must distinguish absent-slave from other error conditions; a sentinel enables precise error handling.

requirements.title: New returns initialised bus
requirements.description: virtual.New shall return a Bus with an empty response table, no subscribers, and nil error.
requirements.rationale: Deterministic initial state required for predictable test behaviour.

requirements.title: Publish stores response
requirements.description: Publish(id, data) shall atomically store a copy of data as the response for id, replacing any prior entry.
requirements.rationale: A stale or partial response must never be served; atomic replacement ensures consistency.

requirements.title: Publish(nil) removes response
requirements.description: Publish(id, nil) shall remove the registered response for id so that subsequent SendHeader returns ErrNoResponse.
requirements.rationale: Slaves must be able to withdraw a response (e.g., during sleep mode).

requirements.title: Publish rejects ID > MaxID
requirements.description: Publish shall return an error when id > 0x3F.
requirements.rationale: REQ-LIN-001 boundary enforced at the transport layer.

requirements.title: Publish after Close returns error
requirements.description: Publish shall return an error when called after Close.
requirements.rationale: Operations on a closed bus must fail deterministically, not silently succeed.

requirements.title: SendHeader computes correct PID
requirements.description: SendHeader shall set Frame.ID to the requested id and compute the PID using ProtectID before broadcasting.
requirements.rationale: SG-01: the PID in the synthesised frame must reflect ProtectID output.

requirements.title: SendHeader computes correct checksum
requirements.description: SendHeader shall set Frame.Checksum using CalcChecksum with the response's ChecksumType.
requirements.rationale: SG-02: checksum in the synthesised frame must be verifiable by subscribers.

requirements.title: SendHeader broadcasts to subscribers
requirements.description: SendHeader shall deliver the synthesised Frame to every subscriber whose filter matches the frame ID.
requirements.rationale: All observers must receive the same frame data.

requirements.title: SendHeader returns ErrNoResponse
requirements.description: SendHeader shall return ErrNoResponse when no response is registered for the requested id.
requirements.rationale: REQ-LIN-014 enforced at the virtual transport layer.

requirements.title: SendHeader rejects ID > MaxID
requirements.description: SendHeader shall return an error when id > 0x3F.
requirements.rationale: REQ-LIN-001 boundary enforced at the transport layer.

requirements.title: Subscribe exact filter isolates by ID
requirements.description: A subscriber with Filter{ID: x} shall not receive frames whose ID differs from x.
requirements.rationale: SG-05: cross-frame-ID leakage would cause application code to act on the wrong data.

requirements.title: Subscribe All filter receives every frame
requirements.description: A subscriber with Filter{All: true} shall receive every frame regardless of ID.
requirements.rationale: Bus monitoring requires all-frames subscription.

requirements.title: Full subscriber channel drops frames without blocking
requirements.description: When a subscriber channel is full, SendHeader shall drop the frame for that subscriber rather than blocking.
requirements.rationale: A slow subscriber must not stall the master schedule; mirrors real LIN hardware behaviour.

requirements.title: Multiple subscribers each receive independently
requirements.description: When multiple subscribers match a frame, each shall receive a copy independently.
requirements.rationale: Independent observers must not interfere with each other.

requirements.title: Close closes all subscriber channels
requirements.description: Close shall close every subscriber channel so that range-loops over those channels terminate.
requirements.rationale: Resource cleanup; prevents goroutine leaks in consumers.

requirements.title: Close is idempotent
requirements.description: Close shall return nil and have no observable effect when called a second time.
requirements.rationale: Idempotent Close prevents double-close panics in defer chains.

requirements.title: SendHeader after Close returns error
requirements.description: SendHeader shall return an error when called after Close.
requirements.rationale: Operations on a closed bus must fail deterministically.

requirements.title: Concurrent access is data-race free
requirements.description: Publish, SendHeader, Subscribe, and Close may be called concurrently from multiple goroutines without data races.
requirements.rationale: Real-time LIN systems often drive the bus from one goroutine while subscribing from another; a data race would be undefined behaviour.

requirements.title: Publish stores a defensive copy
requirements.description: Publish shall copy the supplied data slice; subsequent mutation of the caller's slice shall not affect the stored response.
requirements.rationale: Shared mutable state between caller and bus would corrupt responses under concurrent use.

requirements.title: Parse extracts protocol version
requirements.description: Parse shall populate DB.ProtocolVersion from the LIN_protocol_version field.
requirements.rationale: Protocol version determines which checksum algorithm and features apply.

requirements.title: Parse extracts baud rate
requirements.description: Parse shall populate DB.Speed (kbps) from the LIN_speed field.
requirements.rationale: Incorrect baud rate causes all frame exchanges to fail.

requirements.title: Parse extracts master node name
requirements.description: Parse shall populate DB.MasterNode from the Nodes Master declaration.
requirements.rationale: Master node identity is required for publisher validation.

requirements.title: Parse extracts slave node list
requirements.description: Parse shall populate DB.SlaveNodes from the Nodes Slaves declaration.
requirements.rationale: Slave list is used to validate frame publisher declarations.

requirements.title: Parse populates frame descriptors
requirements.description: Parse shall populate frame descriptors (name, ID, publisher, length) from the LDF Frames section.
requirements.rationale: SG-03: incorrect frame descriptor leads to wrong signal decoding.

requirements.title: Parse populates signal-to-bit-offset mappings
requirements.description: Parse shall populate SignalRef entries (signal name and bit offset) for each frame.
requirements.rationale: SG-03: a wrong bit offset produces a shifted signal value.

requirements.title: Parse populates signal bit width
requirements.description: Parse shall populate Signal.BitWidth from the signal definition in the LDF Signals section.
requirements.rationale: SG-03: wrong bit width truncates or extends the extracted value.

requirements.title: Parse populates signal publisher
requirements.description: Parse shall populate Signal.Publisher from the signal definition.
requirements.rationale: Publisher field identifies which node is responsible for the signal value.

requirements.title: Decode uses LSB-first Intel byte order
requirements.description: Decode shall extract signal values using LSB-first (Intel) bit ordering: bit 0 of the signal maps to bitOffset in the payload.
requirements.rationale: SG-03: wrong byte order inverts every multi-bit signal value.

requirements.title: Decode returns nil for unknown frame ID
requirements.description: Decode shall return nil when the frame ID is not present in the parsed LDF.
requirements.rationale: Caller must be able to distinguish missing-frame from empty-signal-set.

requirements.title: Parse populates schedule table entries
requirements.description: Parse shall populate schedule tables with ScheduleEntry{ID, DelayMs} from the LDF Schedule_tables section.
requirements.rationale: Schedule tables drive master timing; incorrect delay affects bus load.

requirements.title: Frame() returns nil for unknown ID
requirements.description: DB.Frame() shall return nil when no frame with the given ID was declared in the LDF.
requirements.rationale: Callers must be able to detect missing frame descriptors without panicking.

requirements.title: Signal() returns nil for unknown name
requirements.description: DB.Signal() shall return nil when no signal with the given name was declared in the LDF.
requirements.rationale: Callers must be able to detect missing signal definitions without panicking.

requirements.title: Parse does not panic on arbitrary input
requirements.description: Parse shall return a non-nil DB (possibly empty) and a non-nil error (or nil) for any input; it shall never panic.
requirements.rationale: SG-05: a panic on malformed LDF would crash the application; robust parsing is required.

requirements.title: Frames() returns a defensive copy
requirements.description: DB.Frames() shall return a new map; mutations to the returned map shall not affect DB's internal frame table.
requirements.rationale: Shared internal state could be corrupted by callers; defensive copy ensures immutability.

requirements.title: New returns non-nil Node
requirements.description: master.New shall return a non-nil *Node backed by the supplied MasterBus.
requirements.rationale: Callers must not need to nil-check the returned Node.

requirements.title: Node.SendHeader delegates to bus
requirements.description: Node.SendHeader shall call bus.SendHeader and return the Frame and error unchanged.
requirements.rationale: The master node must not alter or suppress the frame returned by the bus.

requirements.title: Run iterates schedule in order
requirements.description: Node.Run shall process schedule entries in the order they appear in the schedule table, restarting from the first entry after the last.
requirements.rationale: SG-01: out-of-order header transmission violates the LIN schedule contract.

requirements.title: Run calls SendHeader for each slot
requirements.description: For each schedule slot, Run shall call MasterBus.SendHeader with the slot's frame ID.
requirements.rationale: SG-01: every slot must trigger a frame exchange.

requirements.title: Run waits slot delay between exchanges
requirements.description: Run shall wait slot.DelayMs milliseconds after each frame exchange before processing the next slot.
requirements.rationale: Slot timing enforces bus load constraints.

requirements.title: Run invokes OnFrame on success
requirements.description: Run shall call the OnFrame callback with the received Frame when SendHeader succeeds.
requirements.rationale: Application code receives data via OnFrame; omitting the call silently drops data.

requirements.title: Run invokes OnError on failure
requirements.description: Run shall call the OnError callback when SendHeader returns a non-nil error.
requirements.rationale: Application code must be notified of absent slave responses.

requirements.title: Run returns on context cancellation
requirements.description: Run shall return ctx.Err() when ctx is cancelled or expires.
requirements.rationale: Graceful shutdown requires context propagation.

requirements.title: Run returns error for empty schedule
requirements.description: Run shall return an error immediately when the schedule table is empty.
requirements.rationale: An empty schedule is a programming error; running it would loop forever.

requirements.title: SetSchedule rejects empty schedule
requirements.description: SetSchedule shall return an error when called with nil or a zero-length slice.
requirements.rationale: REQ-MASTER-009 precondition enforced at configuration time.

requirements.title: SetSchedule rejects invalid frame ID
requirements.description: SetSchedule shall return an error when any entry has ID > MaxID (0x3F).
requirements.rationale: REQ-LIN-001 boundary enforced at schedule configuration time.

requirements.title: SetSchedule stores a defensive copy
requirements.description: SetSchedule shall copy the supplied slice; subsequent mutation of the caller's slice shall not affect the active schedule.
requirements.rationale: Shared mutable schedule data could cause non-deterministic slot ordering.

requirements.title: Run continues after per-slot errors
requirements.description: Run shall continue to the next schedule slot when SendHeader returns an error (e.g., ErrNoResponse); it shall not abort the schedule.
requirements.rationale: A temporarily absent slave must not halt the entire LIN schedule.

requirements.title: New returns ready slave node
requirements.description: slave.New shall return a non-nil Node with no registered responses.
requirements.rationale: Deterministic initial state required.

requirements.title: SetResponse registers response via Publish
requirements.description: SetResponse shall call bus.Publish(id, data) and track id in its registered ID set.
requirements.rationale: Slave response registration must delegate to the underlying bus.

requirements.title: SetResponse(nil) removes registration
requirements.description: SetResponse(id, nil) shall call bus.Publish(id, nil) and remove id from the registered ID set.
requirements.rationale: Slave must be able to withdraw a response.

requirements.title: SetResponse rejects ID > MaxID
requirements.description: SetResponse shall return an error when id > 0x3F without calling bus.Publish.
requirements.rationale: REQ-LIN-001 boundary enforced at the slave API.

requirements.title: RegisteredIDs reflects current state
requirements.description: RegisteredIDs shall return exactly the set of IDs for which a non-nil response is currently registered.
requirements.rationale: Application code uses RegisteredIDs for diagnostics and health checks.

requirements.title: Subscribe delegates to bus
requirements.description: Subscribe shall delegate to bus.Subscribe and return the resulting channel and error unchanged.
requirements.rationale: Slave observes the bus to monitor master requests and peer responses.

requirements.title: RegisteredIDs returns empty slice when none registered
requirements.description: RegisteredIDs shall return an empty (not nil) slice when no responses are currently registered.
requirements.rationale: Callers must be able to use len() on the result without nil checks.

requirements.title: SetResponse overwrites previous registration for same ID
requirements.description: When SetResponse is called for an ID that already has a registration, the new data shall replace the previous registration.
requirements.rationale: Slaves must be able to update responses (e.g., refreshed sensor values) without removing and re-registering.

requirements.title: DataID embedded in header bytes 0-1
requirements.description: Protect shall write Config.DataID as a little-endian uint16 into bytes 0-1 of the protected payload.
requirements.rationale: DataID is included in the CRC computation and identifies the logical data element.

requirements.title: SourceID embedded in header bytes 2-3
requirements.description: Protect shall write Config.SourceID as a little-endian uint16 into bytes 2-3 of the protected payload.
requirements.rationale: SourceID is included in the CRC computation and identifies the sender node.

requirements.title: SequenceCounter starts at 0 and increments
requirements.description: The Protector's SequenceCounter shall start at 0 and increment by 1 with each Protect call.
requirements.rationale: Monotonically increasing counter enables detection of missed or replayed frames.

requirements.title: SequenceCounter embedded in header bytes 4-7
requirements.description: Protect shall write the current SequenceCounter as a little-endian uint32 into bytes 4-7.
requirements.rationale: SG-04: counter must be present in the wire format for gap detection.

requirements.title: CRC computed over header and payload
requirements.description: Protect shall compute CRC-16/CCITT-FALSE (poly=0x1021, init=0xFFFF) over the header with CRC slot zeroed, concatenated with the payload.
requirements.rationale: SG-04: CRC must cover all bytes including DataID and SourceID.

requirements.title: CRC embedded in header bytes 8-9
requirements.description: Protect shall write the computed CRC as a little-endian uint16 into bytes 8-9.
requirements.rationale: SG-04: CRC must be transmitted so the receiver can verify it.

requirements.title: Unwrap returns ErrHeaderTooShort for payload < 10 bytes
requirements.description: Unwrap shall return E2EError{Kind: ErrHeaderTooShort} when the input is shorter than 10 bytes.
requirements.rationale: SG-04: truncated header cannot be validated; must be rejected.

requirements.title: Unwrap detects byte corruption via CRC
requirements.description: Unwrap shall return E2EError{Kind: ErrCRCMismatch} when the recomputed CRC does not match bytes 8-9.
requirements.rationale: SG-04: any single-byte corruption in header or payload must be detected.

requirements.title: Unwrap detects sequence gap
requirements.description: Unwrap shall return E2EError{Kind: ErrSequenceGap} when the received counter is not exactly lastSeq+1 (after the first received message).
requirements.rationale: SG-04: a gap indicates a missed or replayed frame.

requirements.title: Unwrap returns original payload on success
requirements.description: Unwrap shall strip the 10-byte header and return a copy of the original payload when all checks pass.
requirements.rationale: Application code must receive the original payload unchanged.

requirements.title: Protect/Unwrap round-trip preserves payload
requirements.description: For any payload p, Unwrap(Protect(p)) shall return a byte-for-byte copy of p with nil error when Protector and Receiver share the same Config.
requirements.rationale: End-to-end correctness: protection must be lossless.

requirements.title: Protect output length equals 10 + len(payload)
requirements.description: The byte slice returned by Protect shall have length exactly headerSize (10) + len(payload).
requirements.rationale: SG-04: receivers must be able to compute the expected output length to detect truncation.

requirements.title: Unwrap accepts first message with any counter value
requirements.description: A newly created Receiver shall accept the first Unwrap call regardless of the counter value in the header.
requirements.rationale: On cold start the receiver does not know the protector's initial counter; the first message seeds the sequence.

requirements.title: Protect is safe for concurrent calls
requirements.description: Protect may be called concurrently from multiple goroutines; each call shall receive a unique, monotonically increasing counter value.
requirements.rationale: SG-04: a data race on the counter could produce duplicate counters, defeating gap detection.

requirements.title: Unwrap returns an independent payload copy
requirements.description: The payload returned by Unwrap shall be a fresh copy; mutations to it shall not affect future Unwrap calls or internal state.
requirements.rationale: Shared payload memory between receiver and caller could corrupt subsequent receives.

requirements.title: Integrating system provides physical LIN layer
requirements.description: The system integrating go-LIN shall provide a correct LIN physical layer (transceiver, break detection, bit timing). go-LIN operates above the physical layer.
requirements.rationale: Physical-layer faults are outside go-LIN scope; must be addressed by the integrating system.

requirements.title: Integrating system calls ValidateFrame on external data
requirements.description: The integrating system shall call ValidateFrame on any frame received from external hardware before passing it to go-LIN APIs.
requirements.rationale: go-LIN validates at API boundaries; raw hardware data must be validated by the caller first.

requirements.title: Integrating system validates frame ID semantics
requirements.description: The integrating system shall verify that a received frame ID corresponds to the intended actuator or sensor before acting on the payload.
requirements.rationale: go-LIN delivers frames as received; semantic validation is the application's responsibility.

requirements.title: Integration: virtual bus delivers E2E payload intact
requirements.description: When Protect(payload) is published on the virtual bus and retrieved via SendHeader + Unwrap, the result shall equal payload with nil error.
requirements.rationale: End-to-end integration test of virtual transport + safety layer together.

requirements.title: Integration: master-slave round-trip via virtual bus
requirements.description: A slave registered via slave.SetResponse shall have its payload returned by master.Node.SendHeader via the virtual bus.
requirements.rationale: Verifies the master-slave integration contract using the virtual transport.

requirements.title: Integration: LDF schedule IDs are valid
requirements.description: LDF schedule table entries shall reference only frame IDs that appear in the Frames section and are within 0x00–0x3F.
requirements.rationale: A schedule entry referencing an undeclared ID would cause ErrNoResponse on every cycle.

requirements.title: Integrating system handles ErrNoResponse safely
requirements.description: The integrating system shall handle lin.ErrNoResponse without propagating the absence of a slave as a safety-critical output change.
requirements.rationale: A missing slave response is an operational condition; the application must decide the safe-state action.

requirements.title: Integrating system routes safety-critical frames through safety package
requirements.description: The integrating system shall apply safety.Protect/Unwrap to all frame payloads classified as safety-critical (ASIL >= ASIL-B).
requirements.rationale: go-LIN provides E2E protection but does not enforce which frames use it; the application must apply it to safety-relevant data.

requirements.title: Integrating system provides monotonic clock for timing
requirements.description: The integrating system shall provide a monotonic clock with at least 1 ms resolution for LIN schedule slot timing.
requirements.rationale: go-LIN uses time.After for slot delays; clock accuracy below 1 ms will cause schedule timing jitter.

requirements.title: Adapt returns a LIN relay.Node
requirements.description: Adapt shall return a non-nil relay.Node whose Protocol method reports relay.LIN.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §10.3/§13.7: the protocol-agnostic application layer must observe the correct protocol tag to route LIN traffic.

requirements.title: linNode.Send publishes payload for a valid frame ID
requirements.description: linNode.Send shall publish msg.Payload as the slave response for the frame ID parsed from msg.ID when the ID is in range 0-63.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §10.3: the adapter egress path must faithfully forward application payloads onto the LIN bus.

requirements.title: linNode.Send rejects an out-of-range frame ID
requirements.description: linNode.Send shall return a non-nil error and shall not publish when msg.ID is not a decimal string in range 0-63.
requirements.rationale: SG-05: an invalid identifier supplied through the adapter must not be transmitted.

requirements.title: linNode.Subscribe converts frames to relay.Message
requirements.description: linNode.Subscribe shall return a channel that yields one relay.Message per received frame, each carrying the frame's canonical ToMessage() conversion.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §10.5: the adapter ingress path must deliver every received frame to the application as a canonical envelope.

requirements.title: linNode.Close closes the underlying bus
requirements.description: linNode.Close shall close the wrapped Bus and return its error unchanged.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §6 lifecycle: closing the node must release the underlying transport so no further frames are processed.

requirements.title: mock.New returns a MasterBus-satisfying bus
requirements.description: mock.New shall return a non-nil bus that satisfies lin.MasterBus (Publish, Subscribe, SendHeader, SetSchedule, Close).
requirements.rationale: RELAY §7 rule 4 / §13.7: a mandatory mock transport must implement the full bus contract so applications can be tested without hardware.

requirements.title: Protocol enum values are defined and stable
requirements.description: relay::Protocol shall define CAN=1, DDS=2, LIN=3, MQTT=4, RCP=5, SOMEIP=6 as stable integer values matching RELAY spec v1.10 §3.
requirements.rationale: Protocol values are wire-format identifiers; changing them breaks cross-language interop.

requirements.title: Protocol enum is an int-backed enum class
requirements.description: relay::Protocol shall be an enum class backed by int to prevent implicit integer promotion and enable type-safe switching.
requirements.rationale: Type safety prevents accidental protocol ID confusion at compile time.

requirements.title: to_string(Protocol) returns canonical name
requirements.description: relay::to_string(Protocol p) shall return the canonical uppercase protocol name (e.g., 'LIN', 'CAN') and 'unknown' for unrecognised values.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §3.1: canonical names are used in log output and diagnostic fields.

requirements.title: Version struct has major/minor/patch fields
requirements.description: relay::Version shall provide int fields major, minor, and patch with equality comparison.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §4: version negotiation requires structured version comparison.

requirements.title: Version::to_string returns 'major.minor.patch'
requirements.description: Version::to_string() shall return a string formatted as '<major>.<minor>.<patch>'.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §4.1: canonical version string format for diagnostics and conformance checks.

requirements.title: Message struct fields: protocol, version, id, payload, timestamp, seq, meta
requirements.description: relay::Message shall provide fields: Protocol protocol, Version version, std::string id, std::vector<uint8_t> payload, system_clock::time_point timestamp, uint64_t seq, unordered_map<string,string> meta.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §5: canonical message envelope for all protocol bindings.

requirements.title: Message id is a string (protocol-specific format)
requirements.description: Message::id shall be a std::string whose format is defined per protocol binding (e.g., decimal integer for LIN, hex for CAN).
requirements.rationale: RELAY §5.2: string ID allows each protocol to use its native identifier notation.

requirements.title: Errc::closed is error code 1
requirements.description: relay::Errc::closed shall have integer value 1.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §9.1: stable error code values required for cross-language compatibility.

requirements.title: Errc::not_connected is error code 2
requirements.description: relay::Errc::not_connected shall have integer value 2.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §9.1: stable error code values.

requirements.title: Errc::timeout is error code 3
requirements.description: relay::Errc::timeout shall have integer value 3.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §9.1: stable error code values.

requirements.title: Errc::payload_too_large is error code 4
requirements.description: relay::Errc::payload_too_large shall have integer value 4.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §9.1: stable error code values.

requirements.title: Errc is an int-backed enum class
requirements.description: relay::Errc shall be an enum class backed by int, compatible with std::error_code.
requirements.rationale: Enables implicit std::error_code construction via is_error_code_enum specialisation.

requirements.title: INode abstract interface: protocol(), send(), subscribe(), close()
requirements.description: relay::INode shall declare pure virtual methods: protocol() const noexcept, send(Message), subscribe(vector<SubscriberOption>), and close().
requirements.rationale: RELAY §6: INode is the mandatory base interface for all protocol adapters.

requirements.title: ICaller extends INode with call()
requirements.description: relay::ICaller shall extend INode with a call(Message req, milliseconds timeout) method returning pair<Message, error_code>.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §6.4: synchronous request/response pattern for RPC-style protocols.

requirements.title: BackPressurePolicy enum: DropNewest=0, DropOldest=1, Block=2
requirements.description: relay::BackPressurePolicy shall define DropNewest=0, DropOldest=1, Block=2.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §8: backpressure policies allow callers to choose between latency and reliability tradeoffs.

requirements.title: SubscriberConfig holds channel depth, backpressure, event_id, topic_name
requirements.description: relay::SubscriberConfig shall hold: int chan_depth, BackPressurePolicy back_pressure, uint32_t event_id, string topic_name.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §8.1: subscriber configuration captures all protocol-agnostic subscriber settings.

requirements.title: SubscriberOption is a std::function<void(SubscriberConfig&)>
requirements.description: relay::SubscriberOption shall be typedef'd as std::function<void(SubscriberConfig&)>.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §8.2: functional option pattern allows composable subscriber configuration.

requirements.title: apply_options() folds options into SubscriberConfig
requirements.description: relay::apply_options(opts) shall apply each option in order to a default SubscriberConfig and return the result.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §8.3: apply_options is the canonical way to construct a SubscriberConfig from options.

requirements.title: SubscriberConfig::effective_depth returns default when chan_depth is 0
requirements.description: effective_depth(default_depth) shall return chan_depth when chan_depth > 0, else default_depth.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §8.1: zero chan_depth means 'use implementation default'; explicit non-zero overrides.

requirements.title: kSpecVersion constant equals '1.10'
requirements.description: relay::kSpecVersion shall be a constexpr const char* equal to '1.10'.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §2: spec version constant enables runtime conformance checking.

requirements.title: error_category() and make_error_code(Errc) are noexcept
requirements.description: relay::error_category() and make_error_code(Errc) shall be declared noexcept and return stable singleton/error_code values.
requirements.rationale: std::error_code comparison requires stable category identity; noexcept prevents unexpected termination.

requirements.title: is_error_code_enum<relay::Errc> specialisation enables implicit construction
requirements.description: std::is_error_code_enum<relay::Errc> shall be specialised to true_type to enable implicit relay::Errc -> std::error_code conversion.
requirements.rationale: Enables idiomatic std::error_code usage: if (err == relay::Errc::closed) {...}

requirements.title: HealthStatus enum: OK=0, Degraded=1, Down=2
requirements.description: relay::HealthStatus shall define OK=0, Degraded=1, Down=2.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §11: standardised health status values for monitoring integrations.

requirements.title: Health struct holds status and details string
requirements.description: relay::Health shall hold HealthStatus status and std::string details.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §11.1: health details provide diagnostic context beyond the status enum.

requirements.title: IHealthProvider declares virtual health() const
requirements.description: relay::IHealthProvider shall declare a pure virtual health() const method returning Health.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §11: optional capability interface for nodes that support health reporting.

requirements.title: Metrics struct fields: write_count, deliver_count, drop_count, bytes_written, bytes_delivered, error_count
requirements.description: relay::Metrics shall hold uint64_t fields for write_count, deliver_count, drop_count, bytes_written, bytes_delivered, error_count.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §12: metrics fields provide observability for bus throughput and reliability.

requirements.title: IMetricsProvider declares virtual metrics() const
requirements.description: relay::IMetricsProvider shall declare a pure virtual metrics() const method returning Metrics.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §12: optional capability interface for nodes that expose metrics.

requirements.title: IDrainer declares virtual close_with_drain(timeout)
requirements.description: relay::IDrainer shall declare a pure virtual close_with_drain(std::chrono::milliseconds timeout) method returning std::error_code.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §13: drain pattern ensures in-flight messages are delivered before close.

requirements.title: Metrics bytes_written and bytes_delivered track total payload bytes
requirements.description: bytes_written shall accumulate the total byte count of all payloads sent; bytes_delivered shall accumulate the total byte count of all payloads delivered to subscribers.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §12.1: byte counters enable bandwidth monitoring and leak detection.

requirements.title: with_channel_depth(n) option sets chan_depth
requirements.description: relay::with_channel_depth(n) shall return a SubscriberOption that sets SubscriberConfig::chan_depth to n.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §8.2.1: channel depth tuning option for subscriber configuration.

requirements.title: with_back_pressure(policy) option sets back_pressure
requirements.description: relay::with_back_pressure(p) shall return a SubscriberOption that sets SubscriberConfig::back_pressure to p.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §8.2.2: backpressure policy option for subscriber configuration.

requirements.title: parse_protocol() throws on unknown string; try_parse_protocol() returns false
requirements.description: relay::parse_protocol(s) shall throw std::invalid_argument for unknown protocol strings. relay::try_parse_protocol(s, out) shall return false and leave out unchanged for unknown strings.
requirements.rationale: RELAY §3.2: robust protocol string parsing with two variants for exception/error-code styles.
